name: Neutrino mass scale: seesaw compression
requirement: Any mechanism for neutrino masses must compress the eV-scale neutrino-mass spectrum into a parameter ratio that does not require fine-tuned Yukawas. The textbook compression is m_nu ~ y_nu^2 v^2 / M_R: three small numbers (m_nu_i) re-expressed as a Yukawa structure (y_nu) at a high Majorana mass scale M_R. For y_nu of order y_t and m_nu ~ 0.05 eV, M_R is forced to ~10^14-10^15 GeV, an independent scale predicted by the compression rather than set by hand.
what_it_excludes: Pure Dirac neutrino mass with anarchic Yukawas y_nu ~ 1 is excluded by the smallness; y_nu ~ 10^-12 is allowed but provides no compression. A mechanism that predicts neutrinos to be exactly massless (e.g., naive SM with no right-handed neutrinos) is excluded by oscillation data.

regime.description: Heavy Majorana scale at which the seesaw matching is performed (~10^14 GeV typical for hierarchical seesaw with y_nu ~ y_top).

parameterization.name: Type-I seesaw block-diagonalization
parameterization.summary: Active-sterile mass matrix M = [[0, m_D], [m_D^T, M_R]] with m_D = y_nu * v / sqrt(2). Block-diagonalising for M_R >> m_D yields light masses m_nu ~ -m_D^T M_R^{-1} m_D.

procedural.predicted_observable: m_nu_i (light masses) = y_nu_eff^2 v^2 / M_R_eff with y_nu_eff and M_R_eff capturing the leading scaling. Equivalent: Weinberg dimension-5 operator coefficient C_W ~ 1 / Lambda with Lambda ~ M_R / y_nu^2.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: m_nu_lightest < 0.8 eV (KATRIN); sum m_nu < 0.12 eV (Planck + BAO); Delta m^2_atm = 2.5e-3 eV^2; Delta m^2_sol = 7.5e-5 eV^2 (oscillation data). For y_nu ~ 1, M_R ~ 10^14 GeV reproduces the atmospheric scale; for y_nu ~ 0.01, M_R ~ 10^10 GeV.
framework_inputs: Predicted mechanism for generating neutrino mass (Dirac vs Majorana)
Predicted M_R hierarchy or single scale
Predicted Yukawa structure relating active-sterile mixing to charged-lepton sector
procedural.compression_ansatz: m_nu_i = (y_nu)^2 * v^2 / M_R_i with y_nu ~ y_charged-lepton or y_top, and M_R_i a single high scale (or two scales for hierarchy).
free_parameters: M_R (Majorana mass scale; one or three values for hierarchical seesaw)
PMNS angles and phases (three angles, one Dirac CP phase, two Majorana phases)
procedural.notes: Compression ratio: three small light-neutrino masses reduce to a Yukawa scale + Majorana scale ratio. A predictive flavor mechanism (FN, modular) further reduces y_nu to integer charges; combined seesaw + FN is the textbook example of stacked compressions.

primary_references.citation: Minkowski, 'mu -> e gamma at a rate of one out of 10^9 muon decays?', Phys. Lett. B 67, 421 (1977)
Mohapatra and Senjanovic, 'Neutrino mass and spontaneous parity violation', PRL 44, 912 (1980)
Esteban et al. (NuFIT 5.3), 'The fate of hints: updated global analysis of three-flavor neutrino oscillations', JHEP 09, 178 (2020)
